title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump Gives Saudi Arabia Ultimatum Before He Signs Major Nuclear Deal
stories.summary:
Trump Gives Saudi Arabia Ultimatum Before He Signs Major Nuclear Deal. President Donald Trump made it crystal clear to Saudi Arabia on Thursday: if the kingdom wants a blockbuster U.S. civilian nuclear package, it has to put its signature on the historic Abraham Accords.

Trump’s ultimatum came after a groundbreaking 30-year Section 123 Agreement signed between U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Energy Minister Prince ... Al Jazeera: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact hinges on kingdom joining Abraham Accords.

Al Jazeera reports that claim casts further confusion over deal to launch US-supported civilian nuclear programme in Saudi Arabia.

links.excerpt: Claim casts further confusion over deal to launch US-supported civilian nuclear programme in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: House passes second war powers resolution to end conflict with Iran
stories.summary:
House passes second war powers resolution to end conflict with Iran. House Democrats on Thursday forced a second successful vote to end the war with Iran, but they did not pick up any new GOP support despite escalating tensions in the Mideast and the recent deaths of four U.S. service members.

Al Jazeera: US House votes to limit Iran war for first time since ceasefire breakdown. Al Jazeera reports that senate fails to pass latest War Powers resolution, in largely symbolic move attempting to reign in Trump.

stories.summary:

BBC: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May.

BBC reports that the price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

stories.title: Why isn't the House Judiciary Committee looking into Thomas?
stories.summary: Why isn't the House Judiciary Committee looking into Thomas?. On Monday, the GOP-controlled House Judiciary Committee — chaired by Donald Trump ally Rep. Jim Jordan — is set to hold a field hearing in New York City called "Victims of Violent Crime in Manhattan." A statement bills the hearing as an examination of how, the Judiciary Committee says, Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg's policies have "led to an increase in violent crime and a dangerous community for New York City residents.".

stories.title: A Pastor Asked ChatGPT For Medical Advice. It Didn’t End Well.
stories.summary:
A Pastor Asked ChatGPT For Medical Advice. It Didn’t End Well..

A Florida pastor has sued OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT repeatedly discouraged him from seeking professional medical care before he nearly died from a pulmonary embolism.

Scott Winters, who led a congregation at a Florida church before his health crisis, claims ChatGPT provided “extremely dangerous medical recommendations,” according to a lawsuit covered by the New York Times. ... Breitbart: Lawsuit: ChatGPT Almost Killed Florida Pastor with Bad Medical Advice During Medical Emergency.

Breitbart reports that a Florida pastor has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging that the company's ChatGPT AI chatbot offered harmful medical recommendations that nearly cost him his life when he suffered a pulmonary embolism in 2025.

At one point, the AI tool told the pastor, "God did not design your body to endlessly fail." The post Laws

stories.title: Thune fires back after White House says Trump’s patience is ‘running out’ on Save America Act: ‘Get the votes’
stories.summary:
Thune fires back after White House says Trump’s patience is ‘running out’ on Save America Act: ‘Get the votes’. Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) fired back at White House after press secretary Karoline Leavitt briefed reporters that President Donald Trump‘s patience is “running out” over the Senate’s inability to move the Save America Act.

Thune was asked by reporters at the Capitol on Thursday about Leavitt’s comments. “She or somebody else ought to […].

Washington Times: Trump wants SAVE America voting rules passed before lawmakers' August recess.

Washington Times reports that president Trump's "patience is running out" with Senate Republicans and he wants to see real movement on his effort to require voters to show photo identification, his White House spokeswoman said Thursday.

stories.summary: One of WA's longest bridges to be built after Indigenous dispute resolved. stories.title: There Are No Ghosts at the Grand – stripping back the physical and metaphorical layers of a haunted hotel
stories.summary:
There Are No Ghosts at the Grand – stripping back the physical and metaphorical layers of a haunted hotel. Renovate rooms, investigate the supernatural and watch people burst into song in this surreal British ghost story A young American man arrives in a dilapidated British seaside town to take on the renovations of an equally run-down hotel, inherited from a mysteriously absent father he never knew.

His power tools talk to him in a Scottish accent, the local people speak in baffling slang, and occasionally people burst into song. Oh, and there are ghosts.

There Are No Ghosts at the Gr.

stories.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI
stories.summary:
Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI. Software engineering was one of the best-paying professions in the US in 2022, but the advent of AI has disrupted it, leading to several layoffs and underemployment Every weekday, Matt, a software engineer, looks forward to his four-hour train commute to Pawling, New York.

It’s time he uses to work on his own project: a browser-based video game for which he writes every line of code himself. “I am actively trying to keep my axe sharp,” said Matt, who did not want to use his actual.

stories.title: EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps
stories.summary:
EU fines Google €890m for competition breaches over search and apps. Firm told to treat third-party services that appear in its results in ‘fair and non-discriminatory manner’ Google has been fined a total of €890m (£760m) by the EU for breaches of online competition laws by its search and app store services.

The European Commission, the EU’s executive arm, said Google had broken the Digital Markets Act by giving priority to its own services, such as shopping and hotel deals, in search results over those of its rivals. <a href="https://www.theg.

stories.title: Social Darwinists to tech bros: tracing the long fight against equality in US
stories.summary:
Social Darwinists to tech bros: tracing the long fight against equality in US.

Historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s book Country of Lords looks back at the many men who have tried to fight against egalitarianism, from John Adams to Peter Thiel The historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s new book is published amid celebrations of the 250th anniversary of the declaration of independence and its truth “self-evident, that all men are created equal”.

Country of Lords, however, is a history of Neo-Aristocrats, Socia.

stories.title: P5.5-M shabu, smuggled cigarettes seized in Lanao del Sur
stories.summary:
P5.5-M shabu, smuggled cigarettes seized in Lanao del Sur. COTABATO CITY — Some P5.5 million worth of suspected shabu (methamphetamine hydrochloride) and smuggled cigarettes were seized by the Police Regional Office–Bangsamoro Autonomous Region (PRO-BAR) in two separate law enforcement operations in Lanao del Sur on Wednesday, July 22.

Two suspects were arrested. In a buy-bust operation conducted in Barangay Lalabuan, Balindong (Wato), authorities arrested.

stories.title: Taiwan extends microfinancing to garlic growers, processors in Laoag
stories.summary:
Taiwan extends microfinancing to garlic growers, processors in Laoag. LAOAG CITY — Garlic growers and processors benefit from a microfinancing program supported by Taiwan, providing farmers and agricultural entrepreneurs greater access to capital.

This situation is expected to improve productivity, expand operations, and enhance the competitiveness of the local garlic industry.

Eric Pin Yu Chen, representative for the Taiwan Technical Mission in the Philippines, said.

stories.title: Marcos inaugurates Palawan’s first reservoir-type dam
stories.summary:
Marcos inaugurates Palawan’s first reservoir-type dam. MANILA, Philippines – President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Thursday inaugurated the Ibato-Iraan Small Reservoir Irrigation Project, Palawan’s first reservoir-type dam, in Aborlan, Palawan, amid the challenges posed by El Niño and climate change.

stories.title: Angara issues rules for program to aid learners, coaches in contests
stories.summary:
Angara issues rules for program to aid learners, coaches in contests.

MANILA, Philippines — Education Secretary Sonny Angara has issued the guidelines for the pilot implementation of a program that will give targeted financial aid to students and teacher-coaches participating in national and international competitions endorsed or acknowledged by the Department of Education (DepEd) for the school year 2026–2027.

The program is called the Government Assistance.

stories.title: Protected wild birds rescued from illegal captivity in Sorsogon
stories.summary:
Protected wild birds rescued from illegal captivity in Sorsogon. LEGAZPI CITY — Authorities rescued 17 protected wildlife, including two Philippine long-tailed macaques and 15 native birds, from a private residence in Sorsogon City where they had been illegally kept in captivity.

The Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Office (PENRO) in Sorsogon conducted the rescue operation on Wednesday (July 22) in Sitio Madan-an, Barangay Bibincahan,.
